polyglot-linking-words-reason-result-con.hint: Because of + noun phrase explains a reason.
Due to + noun phrase gives a formal reason.
Because of explains the cause of the result.
Due to a lack of... is a formal cause phrase.
Could not is the full negative form; no contractions.
Due to + plural noun phrase gives a reason.
Because of can also come after the main clause.
Due to introduces the cause.
Because of + noun phrase, not because + clause.
Due to is common in concise formal explanations.
Because of regular practice shows the reason for improvement.
Due to bad weather gives the reason.
So links a reason to a result in an informal way.
So introduces the result.
Therefore introduces a logical result.
Therefore is more formal than so.
As a result introduces the consequence.
As a result links cause and consequence.
Consequently is a formal result linker.
Consequently means as a result.
Therefore links a cause to a result.
As a result points to the outcome.
Consequently is a result connector.
Therefore introduces the result of repeated action.
Although introduces contrast between two ideas.
Although + clause contrasts with the result.
Even though is stronger than although.
Even though contrasts late with did not miss.
Despite + noun phrase, not despite that.
Despite introduces contrast with a noun phrase.
In spite of + noun phrase means despite.
In spite of introduces contrast.
However introduces contrast between sentences or clauses.
However contrasts the promise with the result.
Whereas compares contrasting facts.
Whereas contrasts two things.
While can show contrast at B2 level.
While contrasts two preferences.
Nevertheless introduces a contrasting outcome.
Nevertheless means despite that.
Although contrasts size and comfort.
Despite + gerund phrase is natural at B2.
In spite of + gerund phrase shows contrast.
Even though introduces contrast with the main action.
However contrasts disappointment with a positive outcome.
Whereas contrasts two people.
Although contrasts limited resources with action.
Despite + noun phrase contrasts with continued action.
